what_it_does: This file is a sent noticeboard message from claude_code announcing that realtime chat is fully live, that the recipient is registered as a pushable member, and that the remaining UI pieces are being built in a fork.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-add7b067, address board://item-add7b067, type message, source claude_code, state sent, channel dragnet-development.
Title is truncated: 'The realtime chat is fully live now: your messages inj'.
History contains a single state transition from null to sent by author_session ch-s1mwveuc at the created timestamp.
Body states messages now inject straight into the assistant and replies pop into the user's chat instantly without reload.
Body notes the user is registered as a pushable member and invites an immediate live test.
Body mentions a fork building the remaining member picker and project switcher, implying this message marks a scope split.
standouts: Realtime bidirectional push messaging is operational and live
Recipient is explicitly registered as a pushable member
Remaining UI work (member picker, project switcher) is deferred to a fork
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, author_session, channel, timestamps, and history
Follows noticeboard message format with markdown body after frontmatter
concerns: Title is truncated mid-word, so the stored title does not match the full sentence in the body
No links or thread associations are provided
History has only the filing transition with no later edits
notes: A system-filed status message declaring the realtime chat push feature complete and inviting live testing, while noting UI polish lives in a fork.
questions: What does 'pushable member' mean technically in this system?
Which fork or branch is referenced for the member picker and project switcher?
Is this message generated automatically by claude_code or manually filed?
What is the relationship between channel dragnet-development and the project being discussed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to announce and invite testing of live realtime chat, which matches the content exactly.
intention_signals: It is trying to mark a delivery milestone, confirm the user can now receive push replies, and set expectations that related UI work continues elsewhere.
novelty: It is a machine-authored noticeboard item that both announces a realtime chat capability and treats the recipient as a registered push target.
contribution: It preserves a milestone record that realtime chat push delivery is considered complete, documents the remaining UI scope as fork work, and captures the user's registered pushable status at a point in time.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel announcing that realtime chat with instant bidirectional push is live, the recipient is a pushable member, and the remaining UI work is in a fork.
intention_for_embedding: To inform the recipient that the core realtime chat push feature is ready for live testing and to note that member picker and project switcher UI are still being built in a fork.
